TextENGNew: ``Hello! Thank you all for playing the 'Onikakushi' chapter!@k@vS20/02/440200104. How did you like it?@k@vS20/02/440200105. Were you even a teensy bit scared?``
``A teensy bit doesn't even begin to cover it!@k@vS20/04/440400128. Ending on such an incredibly grim bad ending is just infuriating!``
``...So very infuriating.``

``Ahaha!@k@vS20/02/440200107. This 'Onikakushi' chapter is the introductory story for those challenging the overarching mystery.@k@vS20/02/440200108. So it's basically a prologue to everything that follows!``
``And this prologue... ends with such a devastating bad ending?``
changeReason: New translation changes 'How could the prologue... have such an incredibly dark ending?' to 'And this prologue... ends with such a devastating bad ending?' - slightly different framing.

TextENGNew: ``Hmm, but isn't that how it sometimes works?@k@vS20/03/440300250. The player explores the story looking for ways to avoid the worst possible bad ending!``
``...It's only just begun. I'm looking forward to the next story.``
``I wonder what kind of stories lie ahead?@k@vS20/03/440300252. Rena, have you heard anything?``
``Yeah.@k@vS20/02/440200110. This episode featured Rena as your closest companion,@k@vS20/02/440200111. but there will also be scenarios centered around Mii-chan, Satoko-chan, and others!``
``What kinds of developments do the other scenarios have?``

``In this 'Onikakushi' chapter, we were introduced to the chain of mysterious deaths that have occurred in Hinamizawa recently, right?@k@vS20/02/440200116. In Mii-chan's scenario, for instance, the dark side of Hinamizawa and the mystery of Oyashiro-sama come into sharper focus!``

``With all those strange events piling up, I got completely lost... what exactly is Oyashiro-sama's curse?@k@vS20/03/440300256. Or rather, does Oyashiro-sama even exist? I think we'll finally start getting some answers.``
``......Those about to tackle the other scenarios may be surprised by the sheer scope and unexpectedness of what lies ahead.``
``...Hmm, I haven't heard the details myself...@k@vS20/02/440200118. ......But... I have a bad feeling about it.``
``......It sounds like another unsettling story where someone gets cursed.``
``Tough break for Kei-chan, huh~!@k@vS20/03/440300258. I wonder what kind of curse he'll get hit with this time!``
``Still, I'm quite looking forward to seeing what sort of story it will be!``
``Sorry, sorry, sorry, sorry for being late.``
``Hey, everyone!@k@vS20/08/440800066. Great work out there!``
``Same to you~!!@k@vS20/03/440300260. Hm? Where's Kei-chan?``
``Keiichi-kun already headed off to do a script read-through for the next chapter.@k@vS20/08/440800068. Being the protagonist is tough work!``
``...Oh, really?@k@vS20/02/440200120. That's too bad.@k@vS20/02/440200121. He could have at least shown his face for a bit.``
``......He probably had so many lines that he had to stay behind to finish recording.``
``Well, you can hardly blame him, Furude-san.@k@vS20/11/440700337. Maebara-san alone has enough dialogue to fill an entire adventure game's full cast. Hahaha!``
``But still... it was a really rough story, especially for Keiichi-kun and Rena-chan!``
``Rough doesn't even cover it! It was the bad ending to end all bad endings!``
``In the end... what was the true identity of the thing that attacked Keiichi-kun...?``
``My theory is that it was the work of people, disguised to look like a curse.``
changeReason: New translation changes from 'the identity of the thing that attacked Keiichi-kun' to 'the true identity of the thing that attacked Keiichi-kun' adding 正体 emphasis.

TextENGNew: ``......But Keiichi made that mysterious phone call right at the end.``

``Yeah.@k@vS20/03/440300262. I thought that call was strange too.@k@vS20/03/440300263. At that point, Kei-chan was certain the culprit was human, right? But the phone call sounded like he was being chased by something completely inhuman!``
``......That really was an odd phone call.@k@vS20/04/440400135. It was almost like he was being chased by a ghost or something.``
``Just from that call alone... you'd really think the culprit wasn't human at all, but the curse.``
changeReason: New translation 'you'd really think the culprit wasn't human at all, but the curse' more clearly articulates the conclusion vs original's 'it really makes me think that it was the work of a curse after all.'

TextENGNew: ``Since we're all here, why don't we share our theories?@k@vS20/11/440700340. Let's start with Sonozaki-san.``
``I'm absolutely on team 'human culprit'!@k@vS20/03/440300265. I think someone is staging these incidents to mirror some creepy old legend of Hinamizawa!``
``That's true.@k@vS20/02/440200125. It is pretty suspicious that mysterious deaths keep happening every year, right on the day of the festival.``
``Those chain of deaths are someone reenacting some old tale. I'm sure of it!``

``I think it was the curse!!@k@vS20/04/440400137. If you look at the scene where Keiichi-san went berserk at the front door, or the final phone call, there's no way a human culprit explains any of that!``
changeReason: New translation changes from 'the scene were Keiichi-san went berserk' (typo 'were' for 'where') to 'the scene where Keiichi-san went berserk at the front door,' adding location detail.

TextENGNew: ``Now that you mention it, that's true...@k@vS20/02/440200127. Those really didn't look like scenes that could be the work of a human.``

``...Hmm, but I still... think it was a person.@k@vS20/08/440800072. Those scenes at the door and the ending were probably hallucinations caused by Keiichi-kun's persecution complex from being cornered.``
``I can't understand when you use such complicated words!!``
``......In other words, Keiichi got so scared that he was seeing things.``
``Th-then, then, THEN... what was really happening in that scene...? What was that mysterious presence behind Keiichi-san?!``
``A red herring. A misdirection.``
changeReason: New translation changes 'R-e-d H-e-r-r-i-n-g' to 'A misdirection' - the Japanese ヒッカケ means trick/misdirection, and the spelling-out was a creative addition in the original.

TextENGNew: ``Mii-chan, what do you mean by a red herring?``

``Like old man Tomitake said, that scene was born from Kei-chan's paranoid delusions after being cornered.@k@vS20/03/440300269. By portraying it so vividly, it tricks the audience into thinking it was the curse!``
``Wh-When you put it that way... hmmmm... but, but...``
``Hahaha, well, it just means that's one possibility.``
``In that case... was Ooishi-san's theory from the story correct -- that the culprits are multiple people in a village-wide conspiracy?@k@vS20/02/440200130. What does the man himself think?``
``Me?@k@vS20/11/440700342. Ahahaha...@k@|@y@vS20/11/440700343. Hmm...@k@vS20/11/440700344. Actually, I'm leaning toward the curse theory.``
``Huh?@k@vS20/08/440800075. I was sure you'd be on team 'human culprit,' Ooishi-san.``
``......My character in the story insisted the culprit was human, but if humans really are behind it, there are quite a few things that don't add up.``
changeReason: New translation changes 'During the story, I said it was definitely the work of people' to 'My character in the story insisted the culprit was human' - the meta-awareness of playing a character is more explicit.

TextENGNew: ``Like what?@k@vS20/02/440200132. Please share your deduction, Detective Ooishi-san...!``
``First of all, if a human is the culprit, there absolutely must be a motive.@k@vS20/11/440700347. ...The question is: who stands to gain from all of this?``
``So you're saying, if it's humans, then someone has to benefit from causing this chain of mysterious deaths?``

``Exactly.@k@vS20/11/440700349. While there were clear interests at stake when dam-related individuals were dying, the recent victims have no such connection.@k@vS20/11/440700350. In the absence of a motive, it becomes hard to insist on a human culprit.``
changeReason: New translation changes 'I felt that somebody could have benefited from the deaths of people related to the dam development' to 'While there were clear interests at stake when dam-related individuals were dying' - different framing of the motive analysis.

TextENGNew: ``I see.@k@vS20/08/440800077. ...When you put it that way... my argument does get weaker......``
``None of the money was stolen or anything!@k@vS20/04/440400142. There's no way it was a human!``
``But that might not be the whole picture, right?@k@vS20/02/440200134. Like Mii-chan said earlier, what if the act of reenacting some old legend is itself the purpose?``
``Exactly!@k@vS20/03/440300273. This isn't some profit-motivated murder.@k@vS20/03/440300274. This is surely... someone reenacting a blood-stained page from the village's history!``
changeReason: New translation changes 'a murder where you consider the typical motives' to 'some profit-motivated murder,' and 'somebody trying to reenact the folklore' to 'someone recreating the village's bloodstained history through some kind of ritual killing.'

TextENGNew: ``......Then let me ask: does anyone actually benefit from that reenactment...?``

``It's not about benefit... it's something more psychological.@k@vS20/08/440800079. ......I think... it might be some form of revenge.@k@vS20/08/440800080. Revenge for something connected to the village's past.``
changeReason: New translation changes the speculation about revenge, adding 'some form of revenge' and 'something rooted in the land itself' for 土着的な何か.

TextENGNew: ``I see.@k@vS20/11/440700352. Members of some oppressed family settling old ancestral grudges... something along those lines?@k@vS20/11/440700353. Hmm, that's a plausible theory.``
``So what then? Don't tell me you're on team 'curse,' Ooishi-san?!``
changeReason: New translation changes 'A member of some repressed family' to 'Members of some oppressed family' (plural vs singular) and 'settling old ancestral grudges.'

TextENGNew: ``That's right, that's right!!@k@vS20/04/440400144. If the man playing the detective himself says so!@k@vS20/04/440400145. It has to be the curse! Case closed!``

``As Maebara-san said during the story, if we assume Oyashiro-sama was possessing Ryuugu-san and Sonozaki-san to do terrible things...@k@vS20/11/440700355. ......It's actually the most internally consistent explanation.``

``Um... actually, I agree with Ooishi-san on this.@k@vS20/02/440200136. ...For Rena and Mii-chan, who were so kind, to suddenly turn violent... it can't be explained without something like the curse.``
``......This might sound odd coming from the person herself, but... I think it really was me.``
``......I don't quite follow what Mii is saying.@k@vS20/05/440500093. I'd like an explanation.``
``Oh, sorry.@k@vS20/03/440300278. ...What I mean is...@k@vS20/03/440300279. I think this might be an incredibly cruel story.``
``Huh... wait, Mii-chan.@k@vS20/02/440200138. ...You don't mean...``
``When you follow the human culprit theory to its logical conclusion, that's where it leads.@k@vS20/08/440800083. ...I've been suspecting it a little, too.``

``In other words, both Ryuugu-san and Sonozaki-san actually had those personalities all along -- they were demons deceiving the newly-arrived Maebara-san.@k@vS20/11/440700358. ...That's the cruelest version of this story, isn't it.``
``I hate that so muuuuch!!!!@k@|@y@vS20/04/440400147. Waaaaaaaah!!!@k@vS20/04/440400148. Rena-san and Mion-san are both good people! I refuse to believe it!``
changeReason: New translation restructures Satoko's emotional outburst, adding 'Rena-san and Mion-san are both good people!' explicitly.

TextENGNew: ``I-I think so too!@k@vS20/02/440200140. I believe Rena and Mion-chan are truly good people.@k@vS20/02/440200141. ...It has to be the curse! ...The curse!``
``So Rena's also on team 'curse.'@k@vS20/03/440300281. Then let me ask everyone on the curse side:@k@vS20/03/440300282. ...What's the trigger for the curse? Why is it happening?``
``A curse is a curse!!@k@vS20/04/440400151. Human logic can't explain it!``
``Ahaha, Satoko-chan.@k@vS20/08/440800085. Anthropomorphized gods are, in the end, a human creation.@k@vS20/08/440800086. So their motivations will always be rooted in human psychology.``
``...Just look at the Greek gods.@k@vS20/08/440800088. They cry, get angry, feel jealous... they're incredibly human.``
``Um... so you're saying that Oyashiro-sama, like humans, also curses people based on some kind of self-interest?``
``That came out a bit oddly, but yes, essentially.``
``......As they say, 'Let sleeping gods lie.'@k@vS20/11/440700360. Ahahaha, you make a fair point.``
changeReason: New translation changes 'Far from Jupiter, far from his thunder' (Western idiom) to 'Let sleeping gods lie' which better localizes 触らぬ神に祟りなし.

TextENGNew: ``......In other words, whether it's Oyashiro-sama or humans, there has to be a reason behind it.``
``But Rika,@k@vS20/04/440400153. if that reason isn't clear... where does that leave us?``
``......It means we simply don't know yet.``
changeReason: New translation changes 'I don't know' to 'It means we simply don't know yet' - adding 'yet' changes the implication significantly.

TextENGNew: ``Ahahahahaha!@k@vS20/02/440200144. That's right.@k@vS20/02/440200145. This is still just the prologue, after all.@k@vS20/02/440200146. I think the mystery will become clearer as we experience more of the stories!``
``That's right.@k@vS20/08/440800091. It's difficult to theorize much further based on what we have so far.``
``No way!@k@vS20/03/440300286. I won't be satisfied until we've settled this!``
``My, Mion-san is quite particular about this.``
``Ahahaha!@k@vS20/03/440300288. Maximum deduction from limited information!!@k@vS20/03/440300289. It's kind of fun when you think of it like club activities, isn't it?``
``Oh, yeah!@k@vS20/02/440200148. When you put it that way, it is kind of fun!``
``Well then, let's summarize.@k@vS20/11/440700362. Let's see, I'm on team 'curse.'@k@vS20/11/440700363. Tomitake-san is on team 'human culprit,' right?``
``Yes.@k@vS20/08/440800093. I'll stick with the human culprit theory.``
``I'm also on team 'human culprit'!@k@vS20/03/440300291. Not that I dislike the fantasy of a curse, mind you.@k@vS20/03/440300292. But when it's club time, you gotta stay cool and analytical!``
``That's two for team 'human.'@k@vS20/11/440700365. Ryuugu-san, you were on team 'curse,' correct?``
changeReason: New translation changes 'This isn't a murder where you consider the typical motives' to 'This isn't some profit-motivated murder' and expands the ritual killing theory.

TextENGNew: ``Yes.@k@vS20/02/440200150. Satoko-chan and I are on team 'curse.'@k@vS20/02/440200151. Right?``
``That's right!@k@vS20/04/440400156. Team 'curse'!@k@vS20/04/440400157. Anyone who'd call Rena-san and Mion-san demons is the real meanie!``
``Well... I don't want to believe my own character is some kind of demon either.@k@vS20/03/440300294. ......But when it's club time, you gotta stay cool!``
``Now, now.@k@vS20/11/440700367. It doesn't matter which side you choose.``
changeReason: New translation restructures Mion's club mode speech, adding 'when it's club time, you've got to stay cool!' and 'Picking sides based on emotion will get you burned!'

TextENGNew: ``Oh? Rika-chan hasn't weighed in yet.@k@vS20/02/440200153. What do you think?``
``......I don't think Oyashiro-sama is an evil god.``
``Oh? That's an interesting take.``
``......I wouldn't want to worship a god that only curses people.@k@vS20/05/440500098. I much prefer a god that bestows blessings.``
``That's right -- Rika-chan serves as a shrine maiden, doesn't she...!@k@vS20/02/440200155. Yeah. That's a good perspective.``
``So what then?@k@vS20/03/440300297. If Rika-chan doesn't think it was Oyashiro-sama... does that mean she thinks the culprit is human?!``
``......Let's go with that.``
``Well, well.@k@vS20/11/440700369. ...The votes are split right down the middle!@k@vS20/11/440700370. Team 'curse' and team 'human' are tied.``
``What do we do now?@k@vS20/03/440300299. It's a dead tie.@k@vS20/03/440300300. No resolution -- that's no fun at all!``
``There's a reason courts have an odd number of judges, you know.@k@vS20/08/440800096. We need one more opinion to break the tie!``
changeReason: New translation changes 'We need there to be an odd number of judges, in other words' to 'There's a reason courts have an odd number of judges' - more natural phrasing.

TextENGNew: ``Oh, then this works out perfectly.@k@vS20/02/440200157. Why don't we ask Keiichi-kun himself?@k@vS20/02/440200158. Over the phone!``
``......The opinion of the person who was actually killed by the curse is invaluable.``
changeReason: New translation changes 'The opinion of the victim himself is very valuable' to 'The opinion of the person who was actually killed by the curse is invaluable' - specifying 'killed by the curse' adds characterization.

TextENGNew: ``If that's what we're doing, let's call him right away!``
``Hello, Keiichi-kun?@k@vS20/02/440200160. It's Rena.@k@vS20/02/440200161. Are you busy with work right now?``
``Oh man, I'm swamped.@k@vS20/01/440100193. I thought I'd get a breather after finishing one scenario, but they're throwing the next one at me already!``
``How's Kei-chan?@k@vS20/03/440300302. Busy?``
``I can hear you, you know!!@k@vS20/01/440100195. Must be nice having a wrap party while I'm stuck here!@k@vS20/01/440100196. I wanted to come too!``
``......If any of us ends up with more remaining lines to record than Keiichi, then he can join.``
changeReason: New translation changes 'If one us manages to surpasses him' (grammar error) to 'If any of us ends up with more remaining lines to record than Keiichi' - clearer and corrected.

TextENGNew: ``Rika, with that volume of lines, that's utterly impossible...``
``Oh, sorry about that, Keiichi-kun.@k@vS20/02/440200165. ...So about this scenario.@k@vS20/02/440200166. Keiichi-kun,@k@vS20/02/440200167. what do you think the thing that killed you was?``
``Basically, we're deadlocked on whether Kei-chan was killed by humans or by Oyashiro-sama's curse.``
``I'm really sorry!@k@vS20/02/440200170. I know you're busy with the script read-through...@k@vS20/02/440200171. What does Keiichi-kun think?``
``Isn't it obvious?!``
``Which one?``

``It's not a matter of 'which one'!!@k@vS20/01/440100199. I was targeted by shady village people AND cursed by some creepy thing called Oyashiro-sama!!!@k@vS20/01/440100200. So it's both! I got it from both sides! I was the unluckiest guy in the entire game!!``
changeReason: New translation significantly restructures Keiichi's outburst: 'It's not a matter of which one!' and 'I was targeted by shady village people AND cursed' vs original's 'Isn't it both of them!?'

TextENGNew: ``......So very, very pitiful.@k@vS20/05/440500103. Next time, I'll give you a nice head pat.``
``In the end, we couldn't reach a consensus after all.``
``I'll think it over once more myself.@k@vS20/11/440700372. We may have overlooked something.``
``Well then, please continue to enjoy what's ahead!``
